SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS IN OPPOS TO PLTF'S MTN TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES FILED BY DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG , PA., A PENNSYLVANIA CORPORATION LOTSOLUTIONS, INC.
ORDER - STIPULATION AND ORDER REGARDING NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL AND PROPRIETARY DOCUMENTS
ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURGH, PA AND LOTSOLUTIONS, INC.'S PERSONS MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF JAMIE BLANZA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURH PA AND LOTSOLUTIONS, INC.'S PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. FOR THE PMK DEPOSITIONS, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE PARTIES HAVE REACHED AN AGREEMENT ON THE SCHEDULING OF THE PMK DEPOSITIONS. NATIONAL UNION HAS PRODUCED ITS ELECTRONIC CLAIM FILE FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IN PDF FORMAT. AS OF MAY 5, 2014, NATIONAL UNION HAS ALSO AGREED TO PRODUCE ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM, SUCH AS PHONE CALL RECORDS. FOR REQUEST NOS. 2 - 5, 7 - 14, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE; PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED BY DECLARATION ANY SPECIFIC FACTS ESTABLISHING IN THE RECORD THE REQUIRED GOOD CAUSE PURSUANT TO CCP 2025.450(B)(1) AND (F). FOR REQUEST NOS. 6, 15 - 20, GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. PRIOR TO PRODUCTION OF RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER AND SUBMIT A STIPULATION AND PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER TO THE COURT PERTAINING TO THE HANDLING OF CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS. THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SHALL LIMIT THE USE AND ACCESS TO PRODUCED DOCUMENTS TO THE PARTIES' COUNSEL, SUPPORT STAFF, WITNESSES, AND EXPERT WITNESSES IN THIS ACTION ONLY. NOTHING IN THE PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER SHALL DETERMINE THAT ANY DOCUMENT MARKED "CONFIDENTIAL" QUALIFIES FOR SEALING UNDER CRC 2.550 OR RELIEVES ANY PARTY FROM FOLLOWING THE PROCEDURE IN CRC 2.551. ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART PLAINTIFF JAMIE BLANZA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURH PA AND LOTSOLUTIONS, INC.'S PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PROTECTIVE ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-10-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fir Insurance Company Of Pittsburh Pa And Lotsolutions, Inc Person (S) Most Knowledgeable
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Opgenorth, Esq., 415.433.8000, for plaintiff Jamie Blanza.
Stephen Cope, Esq., 213.629.7623, for defendants Alliant Services Houston and Alliant Insurance Services.
Stephen Hayes, Esq., 650.637.9100, for defendant National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg, PA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JAMIE BLANZA's Motion To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fire Insurance Company Of Pittsburh PA And Lotsolutions, Inc.'s Person(s) Most Knowledgeable is granted, in part, and denied, in part. For the PMK depositions, the motion is denied. The parties have reached an agreement on the scheduling of the PMK depositions. National Union has produced its electronic claim file for plaintiff's claim in PDF format. As of May 5, 2014, National Union has also agreed to produce additional documents relating to plaintiff's claim, such as phone call records. For Request Nos. 2 - 5, 7 - 14, the motion is denied without prejudice; Plaintiff has not provided by declaration any specific facts establishing in the record the required good cause pursuant to CCP 2025.450(b)(1) and (f). For Request nos. 6, 15 - 20, granted, in part, and denied, in part. Prior to production of responsive documents, the parties shall meet and confer and submit a stipulation and proposed protective order to the Court pertaining to the handling of confidential documents. The protective order shall limit the use and access to produced documents to the parties' counsel, support staff, witnesses, and expert witnesses in this action only. Nothing in the proposed protective order shall determine that any document marked "confidential" qualifies for sealing under CRC 2.550 or relieves any party from following the procedure in CRC 2.551. Order granting in part and denying in part PLAINTIFF JAMIE BLANZA's Motion To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fire Insurance Company Of Pittsburh PA And Lotsolutions, Inc.'s Person(s) Most Knowledgeable signed in open court. Protective Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Anne Michelle Burr, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.1403, for defendant and moving party;
Ryan Opgenorth, Esq. and Daniel J. Veroff, Esq. of Pillsbury Levinson, Tel. 415.433.8000, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS THE COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG, PA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR# 8098.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Dan Woods, Esq. of Musick Peeler, Tel. 213.629.7623, for defendant and moving party, by Court Call;
Ryan Opgenorth, Esq. and Daniel J. Veroff, Esq. of Pillsbury Levinson, Tel. 415.433.8000, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALLIANT SERVICES HOUSTON, INC.'S and ALLIANT INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled and sustained as follows: The Court overrules the demurrer to causes of action 3-5 because plaintiff's fraud allegations against defendants are pleaded with sufficient particularity. Plaintiff alleges that defendants' advertisements over the course of a decade misled him. (Pars. 8 and 13 of the Complaint). The Court overrules the demurrer to cause of action 6. Plaintiff alleges that defendants converted his premiums. A simple accounting can determine the amount of the premiums and it is a specific identifiable sum that will support a conversion cause of action. The Court overrules the demurrer to cause of action 7. Plaintiff alleges that defendants took part in mis-administering plaintiff's claim at paragraph 13. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 8 without leave to amend. The complaint alleges that the defendants were joint tortfeasors and the Prentice rule does not apply. See Vacco Industries, Inc. v. Van Den Berg (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 34, 57. Plaintiff's allegation at paragraph 5 that defendants were acting as individuals does not save the claim; the parties were still allegedly joint tortfeasors. The Court rejects defendants' arguments based on agency because it is premature to determine exactly what role defendants were playing vis a vis the other defendants. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR# 8098.
